description: At end of life the product is collected as mixed construction waste (45.829 kg). Recyclable metal components are recycled (10.565 kg) and non-recyclable components (e.g. plastics) are incinerated with energy recovery (0.54 kg), while the glass fraction is landfilled as a worst-case assumption (34.724 kg) .

At end of life the product is collected as mixed construction waste (45.829 kg). Recyclable metal components are recycled (10.565 kg) and non-recyclable components (e.g. plastics) are incinerated with energy recovery (0.54 kg), while the glass fraction is landfilled as a worst-case assumption (34.724 kg) .

At end of life, steel components are recycled; plastics and sealants (acrylic edgebands, EPDM, PE, TPE) and wood-based components (chipboard and surface veneer) are incinerated with thermal recovery; gypsum-based components (GFP, GKB, GKF, GKFI) and mineral wool are landfilled. Per declared unit: 5.64 kg recycled, 27.12 kg to energy recovery, 0.87 kg landfilled .

At end of life the wall is dismantled non-destructively. Steel components are recycled and minor plastics (EPDM, PE, TPE) are incinerated, while the rest of the product is reused (booked as components for reuse, CRU). Per declared unit: 32.10 kg reused, 1.25 kg recycled, 0.23 kg to energy recovery, 0.066 kg landfilled. This scenario offers the greatest reduction in environmental impact .

At end of life, steel is recycled and plastics are incinerated; gypsum-based components and mineral wool are landfilled; the wall panel with all components is repurposed and reused (booked as CRU). Per declared unit: 26.95 kg reused, 5.61 kg recycled, 0.20 kg to energy recovery, 0.87 kg landfilled .
